parsed.summary: The commit adds an interactive setup menu and renames the npm scope. The changes are confined to documentation, metadata, and a markdown command file — so there is no runtime code to exploit. However, the setup command template has a structural flaw that could cause silent misbehavior on the re-configuration path it claims to fix.

findings.title: Setup menu template lacks else-path guard for unrecognised input
findings.body: In commands/setup.md, the interactive menu template uses numbered-choice pattern (1-4) for the re-configuration flow. If the user supplies an unexpected value (e.g. a non-numeric answer, or a number outside 1-4), there is no explicit fallback branch visible in the template. Because this is a Markdown-driven command parsed by the GLM runtime, an invalid selection may silently fall through to no-op or default to the first option, giving the user no feedback that their choice was ignored. On a setup/configure path this is a trust-boundary issue: the user believes they have reconfigured (e.g. switched models or reset keys) when in fact nothing changed.
findings.recommendation: Add an explicit catch-all / else instruction in the menu template that acknowledges invalid input and re-prompts or exits with a clear message, so the user is never left in an ambiguous state about whether their configuration was applied.

next_steps: Inspect the full rendered behaviour of the setup command at runtime with out-of-range and non-numeric inputs to confirm whether the GLM command runtime handles the fallback or the template must.
Verify that the npm scope rename (@anthropic → @anthropics or similar) does not break existing installs or published package references before release.
Confirm CHANGELOG version bump matches package.json version.
